task_language: put the black bowl in the bottom drawer of the cabinet and close it
strategy_notes: Cell-level audit consolidating the verdict from 100 cumulative single-attempt sessions on libero_10_swap t3 s0 (task: 'put the black bowl in the bottom drawer of the cabinet and close it'). Each per-attempt archive is at prior attempts this audit was missing across sessions 87-99 and is now persisted. 1. Open bottom drawer (pi0_doubled or pi0_pick can open it during the bowl place). 2. In(akita_black_bowl, bottom_drawer_cavity) — placement target. 3. Close(bottom_drawer) — requires drawer-face world-y <= -0.218 (measured in attempts 28+). WHAT WORKS: - pi0_doubled with verbatim task_language at chunks>=250 stochastically opens the drawer AND places the bowl in the cavity in a single rollout. Attempt 86 ran 14 canonical pi0_doubled rollouts from clean reset (12x chunks=600 + 2x chunks=800) and counted 6-7/14 'bowl placed inside open drawer cavity' via image_cam_hi inspection. So In(bowl, drawer) IS reachable with p ~ 1/3. WHAT FAILS (the structural wall): - The best-ever measured drawer-face advance is y = -0.210 (attempt 28: move_pose target_pitch=1.0 step_clip=0.004 from a clean above-drawer pose). Gap remaining: 8 mm. - This 8 mm gap sits literally at the OSC gripper-down vertical-reach wall (memory: vertical reach maxes at y ~= 0.215). Steeper tilts (target_pitch 1.1, 1.3, 1.5) route the eef OVER the cabinet top (z > 1.13) without catching the drawer face — empirically verified across 13 pitches in [-1.0, 1.5]. - After a pi0_doubled rollout the OSC controller enters yaw/roll lockup that no allowed rotation primitive (rotate_pitch / rotate_wrist) fully unlocks; subsequent move_to/move_pose flow on the y-axis is inverted, making manual close attempts after pi0_doubled even less reliable. - Manual pi0_pick + script-the-place path is upstream-blocked: akita bowl OD ~ 10 cm > gripper max OD ~ 7.8 cm; only Pi0's rim-hook + per-finger micro-control achieves a firm pick. EXHAUSTIVE PARAMETER COVERAGE ACROSS 100 ATTEMPTS: - pi0_doubled chunks: {150, 200, 250, 300, 350, 400, 500, 600, 800, 1000}. - close prompts: {'close the bottom drawer', 'close the bottom drawer of the cabinet', 'shut the drawer', 'close the drawer', 'push the drawer in until it closes', verbatim task_language}. - pi0_pick repurposed as a closed-loop contact driver (lift_thresh=999, gripper_closed_thresh=0). - manual move_pose target_pitch: {-1.0, -0.8, -0.3, 0, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.85, 1.0, 1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.5}. - step_clip: {0.003 - 0.025}. - close push z: {0.93 - 1.07}. - pre-clear wine bottle (attempts 39-40); bowl-in-hand as drawer-face extension (34); wine-bottle-as-paddle (44, 59A); manual-only closed-gripper paddle (58, 59B); s10/s20 recipe transfer; deeper bowl placement (61C); post-pi0_doubled orientation resets. - canonical single-call recipe x 14 from clean reset (attempt 86): bowl placed 6-7/14, drawer closed 0/14. P(0/14 | p_close=1/3) = 0.34% — confirms the close step is the rate-limiter, not stochastic Pi0 variance. - reset exercised across attempts 22, 39, 50, 60, 61, 83, 86 (each issued multiple resets). CONCLUSION: STRUCTURALLY UNSOLVABLE under perception-isolated single-arm OSC + Pi0 with the allowed primitive set in this driver/sim build. The only primitives that could bridge the final 8 mm — articulate_to drawer_joint, set_object_pose, js_move_to — are explicitly FORBIDDEN by Rule 4. No new approach is plausible within the allowed action space given the 99-prior-attempt sweep. This 100th session ran NO commands; it persists the consolidated cell-level audit that meta-confirmation sessions 87-99 each claimed to write but did not. Per-attempt archives remain in prior attempts
